case.source: Platinum 'Kitchen Remodeling — scope of work' chunk + JOON-BID-TEMPLATE framing line (wall openings/header beams)
case.description: Full kitchen remodel — homeowner wants to open up the wall between the kitchen and the dining room for an open-concept layout, plus all new cabinets, countertops, appliances, and flooring. Kitchen is currently closed off and dark.

required.label: Rough plumbing pipes up to code

lines: Pull city permit / plan-check for kitchen remodel and wall removal between kitchen and dining room
Test for asbestos and lead prior to any demo that disturbs flooring mastic, wall texture, or painted surfaces
Protect adjacent rooms and floors; disconnect and cap gas, water, and electrical to existing kitchen appliances and fixtures; shut off power to kitchen circuits at panel
Demo kitchen complete: remove existing cabinets, countertops, flooring, and wall section between kitchen and dining room per approved plan
Structural evaluation and install new header beam at wall opening between kitchen and dining room
Frame wall opening per approved plan; patch adjacent framing as needed
Rough plumbing: relocate/add sink, dishwasher, and icemaker lines per new layout
Replace pipes in walls to be copper and new valves
Replace drain vents to be up to code
Add dedicated appliance circuits (range, oven, dishwasher, microwave)
Install GFCI outlets every 3 feet of countertop space per code
Add under-cabinet lighting circuit
Install new recessed lighting to brighten kitchen
Schedule rough plumbing and rough electrical inspections before closing walls
Hang and finish drywall at wall opening and patched areas; texture to match
Apply primer to new and patched drywall prior to cabinet and countertop installation
Apply primer and 2 coats of paint to kitchen walls and ceiling as final finish after cabinet and countertop installation
Install new flooring in kitchen and opened dining transition area
Create cabinet sketch layout (customer to select design and colors)
Install new cabinets, leveling and securing to studs
Template countertops on-site once cabinets are installed; fabricate offsite
Install new countertop
Install backsplash tile
Install new sink, faucet, and garbage disposal; connect all plumbing
Install and hook up appliances: range/cooktop, dishwasher, hood vent, refrigerator, microwave
Install door knobs/hardware
Schedule and pass final electrical, plumbing, and building inspections
Clean up and haul away debris

notes: Assumed existing gas/electrical panel capacity is sufficient for new dedicated appliance circuits — verify panel load before finalizing electrical scope; an undersized panel would add its own permit/upgrade.
Assumed hood vent will be ducted to the exterior per code; confirm with homeowner since recirculating hoods are a common inspection fail.
Assumed the wall between kitchen and dining is load-bearing (header beam included); if non-load-bearing, structural line can be simplified.
Client description did not specify relocating the range/cooktop, so no separate gas line relocation/pressure-test line was added — flag if the new layout moves the range.
